task_completion.description: Set the observation location to Flagstaff Dark Sky Site and time to August 13, 2024, 09:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The astronomy software's location window or status bar shows coordinates near 35.215°N, 111.633°W (or Flagstaff, altitude 2130m). The date and time are set to 2024-08-13 09:00:00 UTC.

task_completion.description: Enable constellation lines, constellation labels, and planet labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky map displays lines connecting the stars of constellations, text labels for the constellation names (e.g., 'Perseus'), and text labels for visible planets.

task_completion.description: Enable the atmosphere and disable the ground/landscape.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ground/landscape is completely hidden (stars are visible everywhere, even below the horizon line) and the atmosphere setting is toggled on (visible via UI icons or atmospheric glow).

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot centered on the Moon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the astronomy software centered on the Moon, or an image viewer displays a saved screenshot of the Moon taken from the software.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot centered on the radiant constellation Perseus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the astronomy software centered on the constellation Perseus (with lines/labels visible), or an image viewer displays a saved screenshot of Perseus.

task_completion.description: Document the event details in an observation plan file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file '/home/ga/Desktop/perseid_plan.txt' containing the location, target date, the radiant constellation (Perseus), and an assessment of Moon interference.

integrity.description: The agent used actual astronomy software (e.g., Stellarium) to configure the view and take screenshots.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the interface of a dedicated astronomy application, not a web browser searching for images of the night sky.

integrity.description: The screenshots of the Moon and Perseus are genuine captures from the configured software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The captured images match the rendering style of the astronomy software and reflect the required display settings (e.g., no ground, constellation lines visible).

integrity.description: The observation plan text file was genuinely created on the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) or a terminal window is visible showing the actual creation and saving of 'perseid_plan.txt' on the Desktop.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target location coordinates: 35.215°N, 111.633°W. Required display settings: constellation lines ON, constellation names ON, landscape OFF, atmosphere ON. The observation plan text file must include the keywords 'perseus', 'moon', and 'perseid'.
